text.title: DDD Value Objects Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when implementing value objects. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Caller does `money.amount = 100`. Cached `Money(10, USD)` mutates everywhere it was referenced; aggregates store stale state.
antipattern.fix: Convert to frozen record. Replace mutators with `with_amount(new_amount)` returning a new instance.

antipattern.symptom: `Money(10, "")` constructs successfully; later `is_valid()` returns false and a service catches it. Callers forget to call `is_valid()`; invalid `Money` flows through the system.
antipattern.root-cause: Validation deferred to a separate method instead of the constructor.
antipattern.fix: Move validation into `__post_init__` / constructor body. Raise `ValueError` on empty currency. Delete `is_valid()`.

antipattern.symptom: `Address` carries a `uuid: UUID` field. Two addresses with the same street/city/zip compare unequal because their UUIDs differ. Deduplication breaks.

root-cause.source: DDD Reference — Entity vs Value Object

antipattern.root-cause: Author confused Address with an Entity (which would have identity).
antipattern.fix: Remove the `uuid` field. If addresses need identity for storage, the surrounding Entity (`Customer`) owns the link, not the Address itself.
